sections.title: When trying to interact with The Nightmare before talking to her

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Hey! Don't just go poking her without knowing what you're up against. Come talk to me first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Nothing good. This dungeon is tainted and has become the new home of the Nightmare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Nightmare?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: A being of pure darkness. No living creature is safe from her hunger. She feasts upon her victims while they sleep, draining their energy without even needing to touch them.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Those that she feeds upon experience horrifying nightmares, unlike anything you could possibly imagine. Eventually, they become too weak to even wake. Death claims them soon after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nightmares? Hence the name?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Indeed. Our kind are not always the most creative.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did she end up here?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: For generations, she terrorised the island of Ashihama, my home. I lost a lot of good friends to her. Sometimes, I even thought I would lose myself. For some, the nightmares become too much to bear.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Some of us refused to give in though. Everything has a weakness, including the Nightmare, and eventually we found it. We drove her off the island, but she managed to endure.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: The people here are ill, suffering from a deep sleep from which few wake. They call it the Sleeper Plague. The illness made these people easy prey for the Nightmare, so she made this place her new home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you followed her here?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: While she still exists, my home will never be truly safe. Thanks to the Sleeper Plague, her power has grown faster than it ever did on Ashihama. If she is allowed to grow too strong, there won't be any way to stop her.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I've managed to contain her within this section of the dungeon. She has no desire to leave for the moment. She has no need to, after all.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: But make no mistake, I won't be able to stop her if she decides to move on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She doesn't look too dangerous at the moment.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Looks can be deceiving. The battle with the Nightmare is a mental one, not a physical one. If you try to take her on, she will pull you into her dream - one where she makes the rules.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if it's a dream, how can it be dangerous?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: It's her dream, not yours. Dying in her dream will destroy your mind, killing you in this world as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds lovely. So how can we stop her?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: If you're willing to help, enter her dream and fight to keep her at bay. Our only hope is to prevent her from growing too strong.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: You can go in alone, but I wouldn't recommend it. The more support you have, the better your chances.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I keep her at bay?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: There are four totems in the area that can be used to harm her. First, you'll need to take down her shield for the totems to have an effect. Once her shield is down, use your energy to charge up the totems.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: She won't sit about while you do this though, so you'll need to take care if you want to survive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what's in it for me if I do survive?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: You mean apart from saving the world? She's claimed many victims over the years. I'm sure she's claimed many items as well. Maybe some of them will be of value to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Works for me. I'd better get started then.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Good luck. Return to me if you need more information.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Sorry, but I don't have anything for you. If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I do. Try to be more careful with them next time.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I think Sister Senga has your possessions instead, actually.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Nightmare.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: From the moment I was born, all I ever knew was the Nightmare. Every night she would feast upon us, filling our minds with horrors beyond comprehension.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: By day, our warriors would enter her lair in the caves beneath the island. We could not hope to defeat her, we knew that, but our survival was dependent on ensuring she didn't grow too strong.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Here's the thing though. Even now, we know little about her. Before she claimed them, my parents would sometimes tell me a story of her origin.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: They told me of a group of shamans who performed an unknown ritual in the hope of gaining great power. The ritual went wrong, though, and instead, they unleashed the Nightmare on this world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you believe the story?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I'm not sure to be honest. I suppose it doesn't matter though. We'll likely never know her true origin. But as long as we can defeat her, we don't need to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Defeat her? Using the totems?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Exactly. Thanks to the Nightmare, the caves beneath our home are mostly unexplored. There's all sorts of things down there, provided you're brave enough to enter.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I found the totems deep within the caves. Someone far smarter than you or I had worked out how to make them. But they perished before they could use them against her.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I managed to recover them, and with them, we finally drove her off the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But she came here.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Yes. And thanks to the Sleeper Plague, she's far stronger than ever before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where the Sleeper Plague came from?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: No. It was here long before the Nightmare apparently. It resurfaced shortly before she arrived. Probably the work of the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Is there anything else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: The name's Shura. I come from an island called Ashihama, in the Pincers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Pincers?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: It's a group of islands within the Wushanko Isles, a large archipelago to the far east. Over here I think the area is more commonly known as the Eastern Lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you're a siren, right?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: That's right. We rarely come this far to the west, the climate is too cold for our tastes. Although while the Nightmare lives, the climate is the least of my worries.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Back home on the other hand, our numbers equal the humans. Sirens live on pretty much every island in the archipelago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why were you the only one to come here?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: We took up arms against the Nightmare because we had to, not because we wanted to. We are a peaceful race, and most were all too eager to lay down their weapons once the Nightmare was gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why didn't you?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: My kind believe in something called Mo Kal. It means that anything bad that could happen, will happen. The others may have forgotten this, but I have not.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: The Nightmare may have left my home, but I won't rest until I know for sure that she can never return.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Is there anything else you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any of my items?

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Doesn't look like it. If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: I do. Try to be more careful with them next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Good luck.

sections.title: Interacting with the Nightmare with items in Shura's Item Retrieval Service

lines.speaker: Shura
lines.text: Hey! I have some of your things. You should come get them before fighting the Nightmare.
